lede: A look at October 29 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Lance Stroll and Lee Chung-ah, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Seoul Halloween crowd crush
events.desc: At least 156 die at a crowd crush during a Halloween celebration in Itaewon district, Seoul, South Korea.

events.title: Hurricane Sandy
events.desc: Hurricane Sandy hits the east coast of the United States, killing hundreds, while leaving nearly $70 billion in damages and causing major power outages.

events.title: Al Jazeera Media Network
events.desc: The Arabic-language news network Al Jazeera broadcasts an excerpt from a 2004 Osama bin Laden video in which the terrorist leader first admits direct responsibility for the September 11, 2001 attacks and references the 2004 U.S. presidential election.

events.title: 1999 Odisha cyclone
events.desc: A large cyclone devastates Odisha, India.

events.title: Galileo (spacecraft)
events.desc: The American Galileo spacecraft makes its closest approach to 951 Gaspra, becoming the first probe to visit an asteroid.

events.title: Margaret Thatcher
events.desc: British Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher opens the last stretch of the M25 motorway.

events.title: Munich massacre
events.desc: The three surviving perpetrators of the Munich massacre are released from prison in exchange for the hostages of the hijacked Lufthansa Flight 615.

events.title: Israeli–Palestinian conflict
events.desc: Israeli-Palestinian conflict: Safsaf massacre: Israeli soldiers capture the Palestinian village of Safsaf in the Galilee; afterwards, between 52 and 64 villagers are massacred by the IDF.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Wauhatchie: Forces under Union General Ulysses S. Grant repel a Confederate attack led by General James Longstreet in one of the few night battles of the war, protecting the Union's recently opened supply line into Chattanooga, Tennessee.

events.title: Mount Hood
events.desc: Mount Hood (Oregon) is named after Samuel Hood, 1st Viscount Hood by Lt. William E. Broughton who sighted the mountain near the mouth of the Willamette River.

births.name: Andrew Ettingshausen
births.line: Australian rugby league player and television host

births.name: Franz von Papen
births.line: German soldier and politician, Chancellor of Germany (died 1969)

deaths.text: Letitia Baldrige, American etiquette expert and author
Chiyonoyama Masanobu, Japanese sumo wrestler, the 41st Yokozuna
Dwight B. Waldo, American historian and academic
Nathan Bedford Forrest, American general and KKK leader
